messages.content: Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 6 smart meters sending periodic 128-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 30 seconds from time 1 to 300, with a reliability target of 98% and a latency target of 20 seconds. The Wi-Fi link has a 100 Mbps data rate and 10 ms delay, and the headend includes a small LAN.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 6 smart meters sending 128-byte reports every 30 seconds over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links have a 100 Mbps data rate and 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay, with each meter transmitting from time 1 to 300 seconds, targeting 98% reliability and 20-second latency. Include a small LAN with 2 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 20 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links operate at 100 Mbps with 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 10 seconds from time 1 to 300, with a reliability target of 95% and a latency target of 30 seconds, and include a small LAN at the headend with 2 hosts connected via a 100 Mbps hub with 6.56 ms delay.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 20 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.256 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 30 seconds from time 1 to 300, with a reliability target of 95% and a latency target of 30 seconds. Include a small LAN connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 20 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi (100 Mbps, 10 ms link delay) to a collector. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.256 Mbps backhaul with 50 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 30 seconds from time 1 to 300, with a reliability target of 95% and a latency target of 30 seconds. Include a small LAN at the headend with 2 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 50 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.5 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with a target latency of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. Include a small LAN at the headend with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 50 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.5 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5000 ms respectively. Include a small LAN connected at the headend with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 50 smart meters sending periodic 1024-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with a target latency of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 50 smart meters sending periodic 1024-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links have a 100 Mbps data rate and 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with reliability 99% and latency 5 seconds, and include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.5 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with a target end-to-end delay of 5 s and 99% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi access link has a 100 Mbps data rate and 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.5 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 seconds from time 1 to 150, with reliability of 99% and latency targets of 5 seconds. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 1024-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with a target end-to-end delay of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. Include a small LAN with two additional hosts at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 1024-byte reports over Wi-Fi (100 Mbps, 0.01 ms delay) to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5000 ms. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend (100 Mbps, 6.56 ms delay).

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.5 s from time 1 to 150, with a target end-to-end delay of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.5 seconds from time 1 to 150, with a reliability target of 99% and a latency target of 5 seconds. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.5 s from time 1 to 150, with a target latency of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending 512-byte reports every 0.5 seconds over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links operate at 100 Mbps with a 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits from time 1 to 150 seconds, with reliability targets of 99% and latency targets of 5000 ms, and include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 s from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5 s respectively. The Wi-Fi link has a 100 Mbps data rate and 10 ms delay, and the headend includes a small LAN with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links have a data rate of 100 Mbps and 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 second from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5000 ms respectively. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 s from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5 seconds. Include a small LAN connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending 512-byte reports every 1 second over Wi-Fi to a collector. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5000 ms respectively, and include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 s from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5 seconds. The Wi-Fi link has a 100 Mbps data rate and 10 ms delay. Include a small LAN with 2 additional hosts at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports every 1 second over Wi-Fi (100 Mbps data rate, 10 ms link delay) to a collector. This collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits from time 1 to 150 seconds, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5 seconds respectively. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 s from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 s and 97% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with three hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links have a 54 Mbps data rate and 5000 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 second from time 1 to 200, with an expected reliability of 97% and latency of 10 seconds. Include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 10 Mbps backhaul with 2 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 seconds and 97% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending 512-byte reports every 5 seconds over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links have a data rate of 54 Mbps and 5 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 10 Mbps backhaul with 2 ms delay, with each meter transmitting from time 1 to 200, targeting 97% reliability and 10 seconds latency. Include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts, connected via a 100 Mbps link with 6.56 ms delay.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 seconds and 97% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with three hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi (54 Mbps, 5 ms link delay) to a collector. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a reliability target of 97% and a latency target of 10 seconds. Include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 10 Mbps backhaul with 2 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 seconds and 97% reliability. The Wi-Fi link has a 54 Mbps data rate and 5 ms delay, and the headend includes a small LAN with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links operate at 54 Mbps with a 5000 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 10 Mbps backhaul with 2 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a reliability target of 97% and a latency target of 10 seconds. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 seconds and 97% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. The Wi-Fi links operate at 54 Mbps with a 5000 ms delay, and the simulation should target a 10-second end-to-end delay and 97% reliability for meter reports. Include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts, operating at 100 Mbps with 6.56 ms delay.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 seconds and 97% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending 512-byte reports every 5 seconds over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links have a 54 Mbps data rate and 5000 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits from time 1 to 200 seconds, with expected reliability of 97% and latency of 10 seconds. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 20 smart meters sending 512-byte reports every 1.0 second over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links have a data rate of 54 Mbps and a delay of 8000 ms. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with a 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits from time 5 to 300 seconds, with expected reliability of 95% and latency of 30000 ms. Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 20 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi link has a data rate of 100 Mbps and a delay of 10000 ms. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 10 seconds from time 1 to 300, with an expected reliability of 95% and latency of 30 seconds. Include 2 hosts at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 20 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.512 Mbps backhaul with 50 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 10 seconds from time 1 to 300, with a latency target of 30 seconds and 95% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 20 smart meters sending 256-byte reports every 30 seconds over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links operate at 100 Mbps with 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 256 kbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits from time 1 to 300 seconds, with targets of 95% reliability and 30-second latency. Include a small LAN at the headend with 2 hosts, operating at 100 Mbps with 6.56 ms delay.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 50 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.5 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with a target latency of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 50 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.5 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5000 ms respectively. Include a small LAN connected at the headend with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 50 smart meters sending periodic 1024-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with a reliability target of 99% and a latency target of 5 s. Include a small LAN with two additional hosts at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 50 smart meters sending periodic 1024-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links operate at 100 Mbps with a 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 seconds from time 1 to 150, with reliability 99% and latency 5 seconds, and include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.5 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with a target end-to-end delay of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi access link has a 100 Mbps data rate and 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 0.5 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 seconds from time 1 to 150, with reliability of 99% and latency targets of 5 seconds. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 1024-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with a target end-to-end delay of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 1024-byte reports over Wi-Fi (100 Mbps, 0.01 ms delay) to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.25 s from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5000 ms. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend (100 Mbps, 6.56 ms delay).

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.5 s from time 1 to 150, with a target end-to-end delay of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with a 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.5 seconds from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5 seconds. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 0.5 s from time 1 to 150, with a target latency of 5 seconds and 99% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending 512-byte reports every 0.5 seconds over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links operate at 100 Mbps with a 10 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits from time 1 to 150 seconds, with reliability targets of 99% and latency targets of 5000 ms, and include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 s from time 1 to 150, with a 5 s delay target and 99% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi link has a data rate of 100 Mbps and a delay of 10 ms. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 1 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 second from time 1 to 150, with reliability of 99% and latency targets of 5 seconds, and include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 s from time 1 to 150, with a reliability target of 99% and a latency target of 5 s. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 second from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5000 ms respectively. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 s from time 1 to 150, with reliability and latency targets of 99% and 5 seconds. The Wi-Fi link has a 100 Mbps data rate and 10 ms delay. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 100 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi (100 Mbps, 10 ms link delay) to a collector. This collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 20 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 second from time 1 to 150, aiming for 99% reliability and 5 seconds latency. Also, include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts, connected via a 100 Mbps hub with 6.56 ms delay.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 s from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 s and 97% reliability. Include a small LAN with two additional hosts at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links have a 54 Mbps data rate and 5000 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1 second from time 1 to 200, with an expected reliability of 97% and latency of 10 seconds. Include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 10 Mbps backhaul with 2 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 seconds and 97% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links operate at 54 Mbps with 5 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 10 Mbps backhaul with 2 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a reliability target of 97% and a latency target of 10 seconds. Include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts, operating at 100 Mbps with 6.56 ms delay.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 seconds and 97% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 30 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi (54 Mbps, 5 ms link delay) to a collector. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a reliability target of 97% and a latency target of 10 seconds. Include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 10 Mbps backhaul with 2 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 seconds and 97% reliability. The Wi-Fi link has a 54 Mbps data rate and 5 ms delay, and the headend includes a small LAN with 3 hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links operate at 54 Mbps with a 5000 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 10 Mbps backhaul with 2 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a reliability target of 97% and a latency target of 10 seconds. Include a small LAN with 3 hosts connected at the headend.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a target latency of 10 seconds and 97% reliability. The headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 256-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. The Wi-Fi links operate at 54 Mbps with a 5000 ms delay, and the simulation should target a 10-second end-to-end delay and 97% reliability for meter reports. Include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts, operating at 100 Mbps with 6.56 ms delay.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with a reliability target of 97% and a latency target of 10 seconds. The Wi-Fi link has a data rate of 54 Mbps and a 5 ms delay, and the headend includes a small LAN with two additional hosts.

Translate an AMI scenario request into a valid XML following TopologySchema.xsd. Output XML only.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 60 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi (54 Mbps, 5 ms delay) to a collector. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 50 Mbps backhaul with 5 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 5 seconds from time 1 to 200, with an expected reliability of 97% and latency of 10 seconds. Include a small LAN at the headend with 3 hosts, a 100 Mbps data rate, and 6.56 ms delay.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 20 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector, which forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay. Each meter transmits every 1.0 s from time 5 to 300, with a latency target of 30 seconds and 95% reliability. The Wi-Fi link has a 54 Mbps data rate and 8 ms delay, and the headend includes a small LAN with 3 additional hosts.

Simulate an AMI neighborhood with 20 smart meters sending periodic 512-byte reports over Wi-Fi to a collector. The Wi-Fi links operate at 54 Mbps with an 8 ms delay. The collector forwards traffic to a headend over a 2 Mbps backhaul with 10 ms delay.
